name: Anguilla
overview:
Anguilla is a small, flat coral island in the northeastern Caribbean — and one of the most beautiful destinations in the entire region. It is known for having some of the finest white sand beaches in the world, crystal-clear turquoise waters, and an unhurried atmosphere that attracts discerning travelers looking for something beyond the ordinary.

Despite its small size — just 16 miles long and 3 miles wide — Anguilla punches well above its weight. The island has developed a reputation for world-class cuisine, with an extraordinary number of excellent restaurants for such a small population. Lobster, fresh fish and Caribbean seafood feature prominently, often served steps from the water on beaches that seem almost too perfect to be real.

Anguilla is a British Overseas Territory, which means it maintains a stable political environment and British legal system while enjoying a distinctly Caribbean character. The currency is the Eastern Caribbean Dollar, but US dollars are accepted almost everywhere. The island has no traffic lights, no fast food chains, and no high-rise hotels — and that is very much by design.

topExperiences.name: Shoal Bay East
topExperiences.desc: Consistently ranked among the world's best beaches — two miles of powdery white sand, calm turquoise water and a relaxed atmosphere that makes it almost impossible to leave.

topExperiences.name: Lobster on the Beach
topExperiences.desc: Fresh Caribbean lobster grilled and served at beachfront restaurants is an Anguilla tradition. The island's culinary scene is exceptional for its size.

topExperiences.name: Sandy Island Day Trip
topExperiences.desc: A tiny uninhabited sand bar surrounded by impossibly clear turquoise water — accessible by short boat ride from Sandy Ground. One of the Caribbean's most magical spots.

topExperiences.name: Snorkeling at Prickly Pear Cays
topExperiences.desc: Two uninhabited cays with excellent reef snorkeling, white sand beaches and crystal-clear water. Accessible by boat from Sandy Ground or Meads Bay.

topExperiences.name: Sunset at Meads Bay
topExperiences.desc: Long, uncrowded beach with luxury resorts, excellent restaurants and one of the most beautiful sunsets in the Caribbean — with views toward Saint Martin.

beaches.name: Shoal Bay East
beaches.desc: The island's most famous beach and one of the world's best — 2 miles of white sand, calm water and beach bars.

beaches.name: Meads Bay
beaches.desc: Long and uncrowded stretch of white sand with luxury resorts and excellent dining.

beaches.name: Rendezvous Bay
beaches.desc: Calm, protected bay ideal for families with views of Saint Martin across the water.

beaches.name: Barnes Bay
beaches.desc: Quiet, secluded beach on the northwest coast — calm water and beautiful scenery.

beaches.name: Cove Bay
beaches.desc: Wild and windswept on the Atlantic side — dramatic scenery and fewer visitors.

food.name: Blanchards
food.desc: Iconic fine dining on Meads Bay — Caribbean-influenced cuisine with an exceptional wine list. Reservation essential.

food.name: Smokey's at the Cove
food.desc: Casual beachfront spot famous for fresh grilled lobster, local fish and cold Carib beer.

food.name: Dolce Vita
food.desc: Italian restaurant with stunning sunset views over Sandy Ground — excellent pasta and fresh seafood.

food.name: Geraud's Patisserie
food.desc: French bakery in The Valley — perfect for breakfast pastries, sandwiches and strong coffee.

culture.name: Anguilla Day (May 30)
culture.desc: The island's national holiday commemorating the 1967 Revolution — celebrated with traditional boat races, music and cultural events.

culture.name: Boat Racing
culture.desc: Traditional wooden sailboat racing is Anguilla's national sport — races are held throughout the year and draw large local crowds.

culture.name: Heritage Collection Museum
culture.desc: Small but excellent museum in East End documenting Anguilla's history, from the Arawak people to the present day.

bestTime.peak: December to April — dry season with perfect weather, busy with visitors and higher prices.
bestTime.shoulder: May and November — fewer crowds, good weather and better value.
bestTime.rainy: June to October — hurricane season with lower prices but risk of tropical storms.

practical.currency: Eastern Caribbean Dollar (XCD) — US Dollars widely accepted
practical.visa: No visa required for US, Canadian, UK and EU citizens. Valid passport required. Departure tax applies.
practical.airport: Clayton J. Lloyd International Airport (AXA) — no direct flights from most US cities. Connect through Sint Maarten (SXM), San Juan (SJU) or Antigua (ANU).
practical.ferry: Regular ferry from Marigot, Saint Martin — approximately 25 minutes. A popular and convenient option.
practical.health: No vaccinations required. Basic medical facilities available — serious cases transferred to Sint Maarten or Puerto Rico.
